claim_policy.allowed_design_claim: Measured baseline adjustment set closes observed backdoor paths, but the primary ATE remains not identified from observed variables under the declared DAG.

candidate_adjustment_sets.label: Naive treated/control comparison
candidate_adjustment_sets.interpretation: Open measured and unmeasured backdoor paths remain; this is association only.

candidate_adjustment_sets.label: Too narrow business-intuitive set
candidate_adjustment_sets.interpretation: This blocks the obvious assignment drivers, but leaves measured baseline paths through platform, region, network quality and behavior.

candidate_adjustment_sets.label: Recommended observed baseline adjustment set
candidate_adjustment_sets.interpretation: This set is the working adjustment set for observed confounding, but it does not identify the primary ATE because latent_motivation remains unmeasured.

candidate_adjustment_sets.label: Oracle set that is graphically sufficient but not observed
candidate_adjustment_sets.interpretation: Adding latent_motivation would close the graph, but this is not an observed adjustment set.

candidate_adjustment_sets.label: Bad control set with mediator
candidate_adjustment_sets.interpretation: This blocks part of the total effect and changes the estimand.

candidate_adjustment_sets.label: Bad control set with support-chat collider
candidate_adjustment_sets.interpretation: Conditioning on support chat opens a collider path.

candidate_adjustment_sets.label: Bad control set with telemetry completeness
candidate_adjustment_sets.interpretation: Complete-case filtering conditions on post-treatment selection.
